channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump declares victory in Iran talks
item.description: President Trump says a deal has been reached to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. NPR's Franco Ordoñez discusses what we know and what comes next.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says a deal has been reached to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. NPR's Franco Ordoñez discusses what we know and what comes next.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Trump marks 80th birthday with UFC fight at the White House
item.description: President Trump is celebrating his 80th birthday with a UFC fight on the White House lawn. A closer look a the political message behind the event and why some Republicans are uneasy.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is celebrating his 80th birthday with a UFC fight on the White House lawn. A closer look a the political message behind the event and why some Republicans are uneasy.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: In Mexico, Juneteenth has been celebrated for generations
item.description: Juneteenth is often told as an American story. But it's been celebrated for generations in Corina Torralba Harrington's hometown in Mexico by descendants of Black Seminoles.
item.content:encoded: Juneteenth is often told as an American story. But it's been celebrated for generations in Corina Torralba Harrington's hometown in Mexico by descendants of Black Seminoles.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Why we're still obsessed with aliens on screen
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Fate of historic slavery exhibit targeted by Trump hangs in the balance
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Trump's order to remove some exhibits at national parks. One historic site targeted by that order faces an uncertain future.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Trump's order to remove some exhibits at national parks. One historic site targeted by that order faces an uncertain future.

(Image credit: Adrian Florido)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Tribeca turns 25 with stars, surprises and standout indies
item.description: From a recording studio inside a Cameroon prison to a tribute to New York's retired MetroCard, the Tribeca Film Festival marked 25 years by spotlighting unexpected stories.
item.content:encoded: From a recording studio inside a Cameroon prison to a tribute to New York's retired MetroCard, the Tribeca Film Festival marked 25 years by spotlighting unexpected stories.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: The secret to better cooking may be in your leftovers
item.description: Fried rice, bread pudding and homemade broth all start with ingredients many people throw away. Life Kit looks at how rethinking leftovers can change the way you cook.
item.content:encoded: Fried rice, bread pudding and homemade broth all start with ingredients many people throw away. Life Kit looks at how rethinking leftovers can change the way you cook.
item.dc:creator: Emily Siner

item.title: G7 leaders face wars abroad and divisions at home
item.description: President Trump and other G7 leaders meet in the French Alps this week as wars, economic uncertainty and strains in the transatlantic alliance loom over the summit.
item.content:encoded: President Trump and other G7 leaders meet in the French Alps this week as wars, economic uncertainty and strains in the transatlantic alliance loom over the summit.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: As Iranians wait for a peace agreement, their economy is fraying
item.description: Many Iranians say they can no longer afford the lives they once had. Writer and historian Arash Azizi talks with NPR's Adrian Florido about the economic pressures reshaping daily life.
item.content:encoded: Many Iranians say they can no longer afford the lives they once had. Writer and historian Arash Azizi talks with NPR's Adrian Florido about the economic pressures reshaping daily life.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center in secretive process
item.description: The removal of President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center after a judge's order was shrouded in secrecy. NPR's Frank Langfitt joined onlookers as workers hid the process from the public.
item.content:encoded: The removal of President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center after a judge's order was shrouded in secrecy. NPR's Frank Langfitt joined onlookers as workers hid the process from the public.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt
